tool.name: Filter null elements

param.name: replacement
param.label: Replace null elements with this dataset
param.help: If provided, null elements will be replaced with this dataset

tool.help:
======== Synopsis ========
Removes null (missing) elements from a collection.
=========== Description ===========
This tool takes a dataset collection and filters out null elements. A null element is a placeholder that contains no actual dataset — it appears when a workflow uses conditional steps and a step is skipped, or when an expression tool produces a ``null`` value.
This is useful for cleaning up collections produced by conditional workflows. Downstream tools cannot process null elements, so removing them ensures the rest of the analysis can proceed.
Optionally, you can provide a **replacement dataset**. When specified, null elements are replaced with that dataset instead of being removed. This preserves the collection size, which is important when element-wise pairing with another collection is needed.
======== Examples ========
**Remove null elements**
Given a list collection produced by a conditional workflow where one branch was skipped::
List: "Workflow outputs"
sample1 → result.txt (ok) sample2 → (null) sample3 → result.txt (ok)
The output keeps only real elements::
List: "Workflow outputs (without null datasets)"
sample1 → result.txt sample3 → result.txt
-------
**Replace instead of remove**
If a replacement dataset is provided, null elements are swapped rather than dropped, preserving the collection structure::
List: "Workflow outputs (without null datasets)"
sample1 → result.txt sample2 → replacement.txt sample3 → result.txt
-------
.. image:: ${static_path}/images/tools/collection_ops/filter_null.svg :alt: Filter null elements from collection :width: 500
-------
.. class:: infomark
@QUOTA_USAGE_NOTE@
